title: Highest Grade For Each Student
description: For each student, find the course with the highest grade. If tie, pick the lowest course_id.
constraints: Ties on grade are resolved by smallest course_id
Each student has at least one enrollment
key_concept: Filter with max subquery, then MIN(course_id) for tie-breaking.

hints.L1: First find max grade per student. Then find the minimum course_id for rows matching that max.
hints.L2: Use correlated subquery or rank with PARTITION BY student_id ORDER BY grade DESC, course_id ASC then filter rank=1.
hints.L3: SELECT student_id, MIN(course_id) AS course_id, grade FROM Enrollments WHERE (student_id, grade) IN (SELECT student_id, MAX(grade) FROM Enrollments GROUP BY student_id) GROUP BY student_id, grade ORDER BY student_id

optimal_solution:
SELECT student_id, MIN(course_id) AS course_id, grade
FROM Enrollments
WHERE (student_id, grade) IN (SELECT student_id, MAX(grade) FROM Enrollments GROUP BY student_id)
GROUP BY student_id, grade ORDER BY student_id;
